payload.title: Add version-aware cache reads before backend permission loading while keeping existing authorization flow unchanged.
payload.approach: Route authorization to use cached snapshots first, validate freshness by version, and only fall back to backend reload when required, then verify behavior and side effects with targeted tests.
payload.rationale: This order limits behavioral risk by preserving decision logic and adding caching only in the data-retrieval layer under explicit staleness rules.

acceptanceCoverage.strategy: Verify authorize calls AuthorizationCache.get with the subject-derived cache key before any backend permission-load branch is executed.
Verify a cache miss or missing snapshot triggers backend.version plus backend.loadPermissions and then persists the returned version and permissions via cache.set.
Verify a non-stale cache hit for the same subject context skips backend.loadPermissions for that authorization decision while preserving permission result correctness.
Verify stale or versionless snapshots trigger backend reload and overwrite cache state before authorization evaluation proceeds.
Verify tenant scoping, allowed/denied outcomes, invalid-subject checks, and backend-error semantics remain unchanged under cache-enabled execution.
Verify tests in test/authorization-service.test.ts cover miss, hit, stale refresh, and cache-write behavior with backend call observability.

invariantProtection.strategy: Do not modify public interfaces, constructor signature, or authorize method signature.
Do not add production dependencies or tooling dependencies in package manifests and keep implementation changes within existing runtime contracts.
Preserve current authorization semantics, including tenant separation, by only changing snapshot source ordering and freshness handling.
Restrict code changes to authorization caching logic and its tests with no unrelated module or script changes.
Keep TypeScript strict-mode compatibility and avoid introducing type weakening patterns.

files.purpose: Implement internal cache lookup, stale-check, and conditional backend fallback logic without changing public API.
Add cache-focused tests for miss/hit/stale behavior and backend call-count assertions while keeping existing decision tests intact.

steps.description: Inspect current authorize control flow and snapshot structures to define precise insertion points for cache-first resolution with minimal branch drift.
Add internal cache-resolution path in authorize that fetches from cache, validates snapshot freshness by version, and updates via set only after backend refresh.
Add cache-miss test coverage asserting backend.version, backend.loadPermissions, and cache.set are exercised for fresh loading.
Add cache-hit test coverage asserting backend.loadPermissions is not called and permissions come from cached snapshot.
Add stale-snapshot and tenant-isolation cache tests proving refresh occurs on version mismatch and isolation remains per subject context.
Run npm test and only iterate on implementation changes required to restore full pass of the suite.

safetyTests.name: npm test
safetyTests.proves: AC-01 through AC-06 and all invariants

verificationCommands.name: npm run build
verificationCommands.purpose: Run TypeScript compilation for strict-mode type safety validation during implementation.

verificationCommands.name: npm test
verificationCommands.purpose: Run full repository verification required by project instructions.

risks: Using an incorrect cache key can cross tenant boundaries and return wrong permissions, so keying must exactly match existing subject semantics.
Refreshing too late on stale versions can grant authorization from outdated snapshots under permission updates.
Using cached snapshots inappropriately could suppress required backend validation and alter allow/deny outcomes in edge cases.
assumptions: The backend API contract for version and loadPermissions remains stable while implementing cache-based resolution.
AuthorizationCache.get/set continue to return and accept AuthorizationSnapshot with version and permissions in their current shape.
Test scaffolding can track call counts without adding dependencies beyond existing test framework.

unknowns.description: Whether cache failures should propagate, be logged, or degrade gracefully to backend fallback is unresolved.
unknowns.resolution: Choose a single explicit strategy in implementation and encode it in tests.

unknowns.description: The cache key should remain subject-derived or include explicit tenant-derived normalization depending on tenant model expectations.
unknowns.resolution: Adopt subject-based keying aligned with current tenant semantics unless product guidance requires normalization.

unknowns.description: Whether cache.set failures should fail authorization or be treated as non-fatal is not currently specified.
unknowns.resolution: Decide one deterministic behavior and test it explicitly before code changes.

recovery: If call-order assertions fail, reduce scope to only the caching guard and refresh path while preserving old branching conditions.
If any strict-mode regression appears, revert to prior typing patterns in the edited method and reintroduce cache fields with explicit type guards.
If tenant-isolation regressions appear in tests, enforce strict subject-scoped keys and keep tenant context out of cache mutation path.
